Defines macros and routines for ECMA built-in object properties and methods in JerryScript.

# Purpose
This code defines a set of macros and routines related to the built-in `Object` in an ECMAScript engine, specifically for the ECMA-262 standard. The file uses macros to define object identifiers, number properties, object properties, and routine properties associated with the `Object` built-in. The `OBJECT_ID` macro assigns an identifier to the `Object` built-in, while the `NUMBER_VALUE` macro defines a numeric property, specifically the `length` property with a fixed value of 1.

The `OBJECT_VALUE` macro is used to define the `prototype` property of the `Object`, linking it to the `Object` prototype. The `ROUTINE` macro defines several routine properties, each corresponding to a method of the `Object` built-in. These routines include methods such as `getPrototypeOf`, `seal`, `freeze`, `isSealed`, `isFrozen`, `isExtensible`, `keys`, `getOwnPropertyDescriptor`, `create`, `defineProperties`, and `defineProperty`. Each routine is associated with a C function that implements the method's behavior, the number of arguments it accepts, and the value of its `length` property. This file is part of a larger system that implements ECMAScript built-ins, providing the necessary infrastructure to support object manipulation and property management in the language.
